description_long: This object resembles a small, turquoise-blue crustacean with a robust, oval-shaped body and six segmented legs curled upwards. The exoskeleton is textured with speckled patterns of blue, green, and yellow, giving it a mottled, weathered appearance. The legs are thin, jointed, and appear slightly twisted, with some darker brownish tips. The head is distinct, featuring a pair of large, dark, circular eyes set on the sides, and a subtle snout-like projection. The underside is paler, with some areas showing a whitish or grayish hue, and the segmentation between the cephalothorax and abdomen is visible. The overall form and coloration suggest a beetle-like or weevil-like crustacean, possibly mimicking a natural specimen found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A small, turquoise-blue crustacean with a speckled, textured shell and six jointed legs curled upwards, featuring large side-set eyes and a robust, oval body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full oval body, distinct head with large eye, and all six legs curled upwards.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the curved back, mottled blue-green shell, and the underside with paler coloration.
description_view_2: Bottom view displaying the pale, segmented underside and the arrangement of the legs and body segments.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the head, prominent eye, and the forward-facing legs.
